title: acetosella albida
intro: Acetosella albida, also known as bog whortleberry, is a low-growing shrub native to acidic bogs and heathlands in northern parts of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is known to have several health benefits, including its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory problems. Acetosella albida can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
